exclusionCategories.rationale: Build outputs and codegen artifacts are derived from production source. They carry no independent logic and are reproducible from source. All currently gitignored.
Test fixtures are data files used as inputs/expectations for test suites. They validate production code behavior but contain no executable production logic themselves.
Snapshot files are machine-generated test expectations. They are updated by test runners, not hand-authored.
Test source files validate production logic but are not deployed. They are evidence artifacts, not production contracts.
Documentation files describe behavior in prose. They inform human readers but are not executed at runtime.
Example and sample code exists for adopter onboarding. Not part of the framework's production contract.
Internal-only scripts that are not part of the public contract. Currently empty directory.

freshnessCheck.method: git ls-files enumeration with pattern matching against exclusion-inventory.json
findings: All 28 exclusion patterns in exclusion-inventory.json have valid reasons from the allowed set.
Zero paths rely on verbal/oral exclusion only; every excluded path matches at least one pattern.
No stale entries found; all patterns either match tracked files or are placeholders for gitignored/future paths.
171 fixture files under fixtures/, 115 additional fixture files under tests/*-fixtures/ and tests/known-bad/.
4 snapshot-format files tracked (*.snapshot.json under fixtures/); no .snap or __snapshots__/ dirs yet.
Generated output dirs (dist/, build/, release/) are gitignored and have 0 tracked files.

coverageImpact.description: With these exclusions in place, the atomization-coverage guard only enforces ownership for production source paths (337 files). All other paths are classified and documented.
